name: Pete Maravich
title: Last known images of Pete Maravich, video stills from a pickup basketball game at the First Church of the Nazarene in Pasadena on January 5, 1988. He collapsed and died of a heart attack about 45 minutes into the game.
description: The last known photos of Pete “Pistol Pete” Maravich are stills from a video of him playing a casual pickup basketball game at the First Church of the Nazarene in Pasadena, California, on the morning of January 5, 1988. Earlier that day Maravich had been a guest on Dr. James Dobson's Focus on the Family radio show. After the taping, Dobson invited him to play in the church gym. About 45 minutes into the game, Maravich stopped, said “I feel great,” and collapsed. Despite immediate CPR he could not be revived. An autopsy revealed he had been born with a rare congenital heart defect, missing his left coronary artery. Maravich was 40 years old. The pickup game was filmed by fellow player Norm Moline, a CPA who had brought a camcorder to the church that morning hoping to get Maravich to sign some rookie cards.
bio: Peter Press “Pistol Pete” Maravich was an American professional basketball player, widely considered one of the greatest creators and ball-handlers in the history of the sport. At LSU he set NCAA Division I scoring records that still stand more than half a century later (44.2 points per game, achieved without a three-point line or shot clock). He played ten NBA seasons with the Hawks, Jazz, and Celtics, was a five-time All-Star, and was inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 1987, just months before his death.
additionalImagesNote: All four plates are stills extracted from the same pickup-game video on the morning of January 5, 1988. Because the exact final frame in the recording cannot be identified, the displayed images are representative of the series.

location.name: First Church of the Nazarene, 3700 E Sierra Madre Blvd, Pasadena, CA 91107
